description: # Task: TASK-9FB44F  ## Title: chcialbym moc miec projekty ktore moge otwierac na roznych urzadzeniach, jesli wprowadze zmiane na P  chcialbym moc miec projekty ktore moge otwierac na roznych urzadzeniach, jesli wprowadze zmiane na PC, po zalogowaniu na telefon moge otworzyc ten sam projekt (bez koniecznosci uploadowania tego projektu).  Zrob Najlepsze podejście: server-side project storage z auth. - przy czym poki co chce uniknac zewnetrznych baz (postgres) - poki co mam tylko uzytkownikow wiec chcialbym ich trzymac poki co lokalnie (moze byc nawet w pliku, ale zeby musieli sie logowac - bez mozliwosci wejscia bez zalogowania)  1. Autentykacja - Schema users już istnieje w schema.ts - Dodaj login/register z sesją (np. express-session + bcrypt) lub użyj zewnętrznego provider'a (Google OAuth przez better-auth/lucia) - Na telefonie logujesz się tym samym kontem  2. Projects API (backend) - Dodaj tabelę projects do Drizzle schema (id, userId, name, data jsonb, updatedAt) - Endpoints: GET /api/projects, POST /api/projects, PUT /api/projects/:id, DELETE /api/projects/:id - data to cały EditorState zserializowany do JSON (już to robisz przy eksporcie ZIP)  3. Auto-save (frontend) - Przy każdej zmianie stanu — debounce + PUT /api/projects/:id - Lista projektów pobierana z serwera zamiast localStorage - PDF-y jako osobne pliki (upload do serwera, przechowuj URL, nie base64 w JSON) — inaczej projekty z dużymi plikami będą wolne  Na ten moment samych uzytkownikow przechowuj lokalnie w pliku (bedzie max 10 uzytkownikow) ; Pozwrol mi dzielic projekt z innym uzytkownikiem (zeby byl dla niego widoczny)  Reszta EditorState (warstwy, obiekty, metadane, ustawienia) idzie bezpośrednio do kolumny state jsonb.                    ---                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Przepływ danych  Otwierasz projekt na PC: GET /api/projects          → lista z baza                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         GET /api/projects/:id      → state JSON z baza                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    GET /api/files/:id/...     → PDF/ikony/zdjęcia z dysku  Zapisujesz zmianę: PUT /api/projects/:id      → state JSON → baza danych (debounce ~2s)                                      POST /api/files/:id/...    → nowe pliki → dysk (tylko przy dodaniu)  Otwierasz na telefonie:  Logujesz się → ten sam user_id → te same projekty
title: # Task: TASK-9FB44F  ## Title: chcialbym moc miec projekty ktore moge otwierac na roznych urzadzenia

criteria.description: POST /api/auth/register creates an entry in data/users.json with a bcrypt-hashed passwordHash field (starts with '$2b$') and returns HTTP 201 with {id, username}.
criteria.how_to_verify: Call endpoint, read data/users.json — passwordHash must start with '$2b$' and not equal the plain-text password sent in the request body.
criteria.evidence: server/routes.ts (lines 18-34) calls bcrypt.hash(password, 10) before storage.createUser. server/fileStorage.ts (lines 68-79) saves the user object including passwordHash to users.json.

criteria.description: GET /api/auth/me returns HTTP 401 with no session cookie, and HTTP 200 with {id, username} after POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials sets a session.
criteria.how_to_verify: Unauthenticated GET /api/auth/me → 401. POST /api/auth/login with correct credentials, reuse Set-Cookie, GET /api/auth/me → 200 with user object.
criteria.evidence: server/routes.ts (lines 56-61) implements the GET endpoint with req.isAuthenticated() check. server/auth.ts configures passport-local and session management.

criteria.description: GET /api/projects as user A does not return user B's project unless B's project has A's userId in its sharedWith array in data/projects.json.
criteria.how_to_verify: Register two users, each creates a project, each calls GET /api/projects — verify only their own project appears. Then B shares with A; A's list now contains both.
criteria.evidence: server/fileStorage.ts (lines 87-91) filters projects in listProjectsForUser based on ownerId or sharedWith array membership.

criteria.description: PUT /api/projects/:id writes a valid JSON file to data/project-states/{id}.json containing a 'layers' array key and no 'pdfFile' key.
criteria.how_to_verify: Create project, PUT a state payload, then JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('data/project-states/{id}.json')) — must have .layers array, must not have .pdfFile property.
criteria.evidence: shared/schema.ts (lines 24-31) defines projectStateSchema with 'layers' and without 'pdfFile'. server/fileStorage.ts (line 130) writes this JSON to disk.

criteria.description: POST /api/files accepts multipart/form-data with field 'file', saves the binary to data/files/{fileId}, writes metadata JSON to data/files/{fileId}.meta.json, and returns {fileId, url}.
criteria.how_to_verify: Upload a small PDF file; verify data/files/{fileId} binary exists and data/files/{fileId}.meta.json contains mimeType:'application/pdf'.
criteria.evidence: server/routes.ts (lines 119-125) handles the upload using multer and calls storage.saveFile. server/fileStorage.ts (lines 134-147) saves both binary and metadata files.

criteria.description: client/src/App.tsx renders AuthPage when useCurrentUser() returns null and renders the editor Router when user is non-null.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read App.tsx — must contain a conditional on user===null that renders AuthPage (or the /auth route exclusively) and wraps EditorProvider only in the authenticated branch.
criteria.evidence: client/src/App.tsx (lines 33-53) contains the conditional logic to render AuthPage/Redirect or the authenticated Router.

criteria.description: client/src/hooks/useAutoSave.ts exports useAutoSave() that guards on docState.projectId !== null before calling PUT /api/projects/:id, with a debounce delay of 2000ms.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read useAutoSave.ts — must reference a 2000 (or 2000ms) debounce value and must have an explicit early-return or condition checking projectId before issuing the save mutation.
criteria.evidence: client/src/hooks/useAutoSave.ts has the guard at line 12 and the 2000ms delay at line 36.

criteria.description: DocumentState in client/src/lib/types.ts contains projectId: string|null, pdfFileId: string|null, and overlayPdfFileId: string|null fields.
criteria.how_to_verify: Read types.ts — DocumentState interface must list all three fields with string|null types.
criteria.evidence: client/src/lib/types.ts (lines 48-50) defines these fields as string | null.

+## IMPLEMENTING — iter 1 — 2026-05-01 17:05:48
+
+### Gemini (Programista)
+
+**Open criteria do adresowania:**
+- `[c1]` POST /api/auth/register creates an entry in data/users.json with a bcrypt-hashed passwordHash field (starts with '$2b$') and returns HTTP 201 with {id, username}.
+- `[c2]` GET /api/auth/me returns HTTP 401 with no session cookie, and HTTP 200 with {id, username} after POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials sets a session.
+- `[c3]` GET /api/projects as user A does not return user B's project unless B's project has A's userId in its sharedWith array in data/projects.json.
+- `[c4]` PUT /api/projects/:id writes a valid JSON file to data/project-states/{id}.json containing a 'layers' array key and no 'pdfFile' key.
+- `[c5]` POST /api/files accepts multipart/form-data with field 'file', saves the binary to data/files/{fileId}, writes metadata JSON to data/files/{fileId}.meta.json, and returns {fileId, url}.
+- `[c6]` client/src/App.tsx renders AuthPage when useCurrentUser() returns null and renders the editor Router when user is non-null.
+- `[c7]` client/src/hooks/useAutoSave.ts exports useAutoSave() that guards on docState.projectId !== null before calling PUT /api/projects/:id, with a debounce delay of 2000ms.
+- `[c8]` DocumentState in client/src/lib/types.ts contains projectId: string|null, pdfFileId: string|null, and overlayPdfFileId: string|null fields.
+
+**Git diff:** .orchestrator/orchestrator.db                | Bin 196608 -> 204800 bytes
+ client/src/App.tsx                           |  46 +++-
+ client/src/components/editor/PDFUploader.tsx | 300 +++++++++++++++++++++++----
+ client/src/components/editor/Toolbar.tsx     | 142 +++++++------
+ client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx            |  16 +-
+ client/src/lib/types.ts                      |   5 +
+ client/src/pages/home.tsx                    |   4 +-
+ implementation_report.md                     |  50 +++--
+ package-lock.json                            | 144 +++++++++++--
+ package.json                                 |   4 +
+ server/index.ts                              |   4 +
+ server/routes.ts                             | 156 +++++++++++++-
+ server/storage.ts                            |  50 ++---
+ shared/schema.ts                             |  63 +++++-
+ 14 files changed, 793 insertions(+), 191 deletions(-)
+
+**Implementation Report:**
+
+# Implementation Report — Iteration 1
+
+## Changes made
+- **Installed dependencies**: Added `bcrypt`, `multer` and their type definitions to support password hashing and file uploads.
+- **Refactored Schema**: Converted `shared/schema.ts` from Drizzle ORM to plain TypeScript interfaces and Zod schemas for file-based persistence.
+- **Created Persistence Layer**: Implemented `server/fileStorage.ts` for managing users, projects, and binary files in a `data/` directory with atomic writes.
+- **Updated Storage Singleton**: Switched `server/storage.ts` to use `FileStorage` instead of `MemStorage`.
+- **Implemented Authentication**: Created `server/auth.ts` using `passport-local` and `express-session` with `memorystore` for session management.
+- **Built API Routes**: Implemented auth (register/login/logout/me), projects (CRUD + sharing), and file (upload/serve) endpoints in `server/routes.ts`.
+- **Updated Frontend State**: Added `projectId`, `pdfFileId`, and `overlayPdfFileId` to `DocumentState` in `client/src/lib/types.ts` and updated the reducer in `editor-context.tsx`.
+- **Created Frontend Hooks**: Added `useAuth.ts` for session management and `useProjects.ts` for server-side project operations.
+- **Built Auth UI**: Created `client/src/pages/AuthPage.tsx` with login/register forms and integrated it into `App.tsx` with an auth guard.
+- **Revamped Project Loading**: Completely rewritten `PDFUploader.tsx` to show a server-side project list, support new project creation with file uploads, and project sharing.
+- **Implemented Auto-save**: Created `useAutoSave.ts` hook with 2-second debounce and integrated it into `Home.tsx`, with a visual "Saving/Saved" indicator in `Toolbar.tsx`.
+
+## Files affected
+- CREATED: `server/fileStorage.ts`
+- CREATED: `server/auth.ts`
+- CREATED: `client/src/hooks/useAuth.ts`
+- CREATED: `client/src/hooks/useProjects.ts`
+- CREATED: `client/src/hooks/useAutoSave.ts`
+- CREATED: `client/src/pages/AuthPage.tsx`
+- MODIFIED: `package.json`
+- MODIFIED: `shared/schema.ts`
+- MODIFIED: `server/storage.ts`
+- MODIFIED: `server/index.ts`
+- MODIFIED: `server/routes.ts`
+- MODIFIED: `client/src/lib/types.ts`
+- MODIFIED: `client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx`
+- MODIFIED: `client/src/App.tsx`
+- MODIFIED: `client/src/components/editor/PDFUploader.tsx`
+- MODIFIED: `client/src/pages/home.tsx`
+- MODIFIED: `client/src/components/editor/Toolbar.tsx`
+
+## Deviations from plan
+- Included `passport` and `express-session` related packages in the initial installation step to ensure all auth dependencies are present.
+- Used `any` for `timeoutRef` in `useAutoSave.ts` to avoid environment-specific type issues with `setTimeout`.
+- Added a `log` function to `server/index.ts` to maintain consistency with existing logging patterns while adding auth middleware.
+
+## Potential issues
+- `express-session` uses `MemoryStore`, which means users will be logged out whenever the server restarts. This is acceptable for the current scale but should be moved to a persistent store if session longevity is critical.
+- Very large projects (many high-res photos) might hit the 50MB limit or be slow to save/load due to the monolithic project state JSON. Moving photos to separate file uploads (similar to PDFs) would be the next logical optimization.
+
+<details>
+<summary>Gemini stdout (raw)</summary>
+
+```
+I have completed the implementation of TASK-9FB44F, providing server-side authentication and project storage with file-based persistence.
+
+### Summary of Changes
+- **Server-side Auth**: Implemented register/login/logout using `passport-local` and `express-session`.
+- **File Persistence**: Created a `FileStorage` layer that manages users, projects, and binary files (PDFs) in the `data/` directory with atomic writes.
+- **Projects API**: Added endpoints for project CRUD operations, sharing, and binary file management.
+- **Frontend Refactor**:
+    - Replaced the initial PDF uploader with a comprehensive project management dashboard.
+    - Implemented a 2-second debounce auto-save mechanism with a visual "Saved/Saving" status indicator.
+    - Added an authentication guard and a dedicated `AuthPage` for user onboarding.
+- **Data Model**: Updated `DocumentState` to track `projectId` and file references, ensuring cross-device consistency without large base64 overhead in the state JSON.
+
+All acceptance criteria have been met, and a detailed summary of the work can be found in `implementation_report.md`.
+```
+</details>
+
+---
+

+
+### Gemini (Reviewer)
+
+**Overall:** ✅ APPROVED
+
+**Criteria:**
+- ✅ `[c1]` **DONE** — server/routes.ts (lines 18-34) implements the registration logic using bcrypt.hash(password, 10). server/fileStorage.ts (lines 78-89) handles user creation and flushes to data/users.json.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c2]` **DONE** — server/routes.ts (lines 56-61) implements GET /api/auth/me with an isAuthenticated() check. server/auth.ts configures express-session and passport for session management.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c3]` **DONE** — server/fileStorage.ts (lines 95-99) filters projects in listProjectsForUser based on ownerId or sharedWith array membership.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c4]` **DONE** — shared/schema.ts (lines 24-33) defines projectStateSchema with 'layers' and without 'pdfFile'. server/fileStorage.ts (lines 127-133) writes the state to the data/project-states directory.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c5]` **DONE** — server/routes.ts (lines 121-127) and server/fileStorage.ts (lines 136-150) implement binary file saving and metadata JSON generation.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c6]` **DONE** — client/src/App.tsx (lines 33-55) contains the conditional rendering logic based on the user object returned by useCurrentUser().  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c7]` **DONE** — client/src/hooks/useAutoSave.ts (line 11) has the projectId guard and (line 32) uses a 2000ms delay in setTimeout.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+- ✅ `[c8]` **DONE** — client/src/lib/types.ts (lines 48-50) includes the requested fields with the correct types.  
+  *confidence:* HIGH
+
+**Blocking issues:**
+*None*
+
+**Suggestions:**
+- 💡 Consider implementing a more robust session store than MemoryStore for production use, as MemoryStore will cause all users to be logged out on server restart.
+- 💡 Add a file cleanup mechanism for orphaned files in data/files if projects are deleted without their associated files being fully tracked.
+
+---
+
+## FOLLOW-UP INITIATED — 2026-05-01 17:39:18
+
+**User instruction:** Follow-up request: when visiting /auth i can just see thepage loading  spinner, nothing more
+
+---
+
+## HUMAN_FEEDBACK — iter 1 — 2026-05-01 17:40:16
+
+### Gemini (Analiza feedbacku → plan naprawy)
+
+**Root cause:** The `useCurrentUser` hook in `client/src/hooks/useAuth.ts` uses the default `useQuery` configuration, which retries on failure. When a user is not logged in, `/api/auth/me` returns a 401 error, causing `react-query` to retry the request multiple times. During these retries, `isLoading` remains `true`, keeping the app stuck on the loading spinne
human_feedback: Follow-up request: when visiting /auth i can just see thepage loading  spinner, nothing more - wciaz nie dziala, popraw logowanie by latwiej bylo zidentyfikowac problem
fix_plan:
Root cause: The application remains stuck on the loading spinner because the top-level AppContent component only handles the 'isLoading' state and lacks error handling or diagnostic feedback for the initial session check. If the '/api/auth/me' request hangs (e.g., due to a server-side deadlock or network issue) or fails for reasons other than 401, the user is left with a spinner and no way to identify the underlying problem.

Fix steps:
  1. Modify client/src/App.tsx to include an 'isError' check for the useCurrentUser query. If an error occurs, display a user-friendly error message, the technical details of the error, and a retry button. This directly fulfills the user's request to make the problem easier to identify. (files: client/src/App.tsx)
  2. Implement a 10-second timeout for all fetch requests in client/src/lib/queryClient.ts using AbortController. Add console logging to getQueryFn to track which endpoints are being called and their resolution status, ensuring that hangs are caught and reported as errors. (files: client/src/lib/queryClient.ts)
  3. Enhance server-side logging in server/auth.ts and the /api/auth/me route in server/routes.ts. Add log statements to track session deserialization, isAuthenticated checks, and storage access. This will reveal if the server is hanging during the authentication check or file system operations. (files: server/auth.ts, server/routes.ts)

Key fix: Add robust error handling and diagnostic information to the main application entry point to prevent silent hangs and provide visibility into authentication failures.
